title: rubus fruticosus var. caesius
intro: Rubus fruticosus var. caesius, also known as the wild blue raspberry or blue dewberry, is a deciduous shrub in the rose family that is native to Europe and western Asia. The plant's leaves and fruit have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming Rubus fruticosus var. caesius include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive function. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make jams, jellies, and other food products.
